issues.title: Linear search inside rendered list item causes O(n*m) complexity
issues.description: Each ProductCard performs `cartItems.find((i) => i.productId === product.id)` on every render. When ProductGrid renders N cards and the cart has M items, this is O(N*M) on every state change. Since CartContext value changes on any cart mutation, all ProductCards re-render and each re-scans the cart array.
issues.fix: Convert cart items to a Map<string, CartItem> in the CartContext (or expose a `getQuantity(productId)` helper). This turns O(M) lookups into O(1) per card. Alternatively, memoize the lookup map with useMemo in CartContext.

issues.title: All ProductCards re-render on any cart state change
issues.description: ProductCard calls `useCart()` which returns the full CartContext including `items`, `total`, and `itemCount`. When any cart item is added/removed/updated, the entire context value changes (despite useMemo, the `state` reference changes), causing every visible ProductCard to re-render even if its own product's cart quantity hasn't changed.
issues.fix: Consider splitting CartContext into a CartStateContext (items, counts) and CartDispatchContext (addItem, removeItem, etc.), or use a selector-based approach (e.g., `useSyncExternalStore` or Zustand) so ProductCard only re-renders when its specific product's quantity changes.

issues.title: Product lookup via Array.find on every render
issues.description: `mockProducts.find((p) => p.id === productId)` is called on every render of ProductDetailPage. While the mock data is small now, this is a linear scan that runs on each re-render (e.g., when cart state changes trigger re-render via useCart).
issues.fix: Wrap the product lookup in useMemo with productId as dependency: `const maybeProduct = useMemo(() => mockProducts.find((p) => p.id === productId), [productId]);`

issues.title: handleAddToCart recreated on every render without useCallback
issues.description: The `handleAddToCart` function is defined as a plain function inside the component, so it's recreated on every render. It is passed as a prop to MobileBar, which means MobileBar re-renders every time even if its other props haven't changed.
issues.rule: Memoization (useCallback for handlers passed as props)
issues.fix: Wrap handleAddToCart in useCallback with appropriate dependencies, and consider wrapping MobileBar with React.memo.

issues.title: handleAddToCart recreated on every render without useCallback
issues.description: Same issue as PERF-004. The handler function is a plain function definition inside HeroSection, recreated every render cycle.
issues.fix: Wrap in useCallback. Although HeroSection is not passed as a prop to a memoized child, it's good practice and prevents issues if the component tree changes.

issues.title: handleAddToCart not wrapped in useCallback
issues.description: ProductCard's handleAddToCart is a plain function recreated every render. Since ProductCard is rendered in a list (ProductGrid), this means N function allocations per render cycle.
issues.fix: Wrap in useCallback with [product, addItem, flyCtx, categoryColor] dependencies.

issues.title: Date object created inline during render for each review
issues.description: `new Date(review.date).toLocaleDateString()` creates a new Date object and formats it on every render for each review card. While not expensive for small lists, this could be memoized or precomputed.
issues.fix: For small review lists this is acceptable. For larger lists, consider memoizing or precomputing formatted dates.

issues.title: No Next.js Image component used for product images
issues.description: ProductCardImage renders a colored placeholder div rather than actual images. When real product images are added, ensure they use the Next.js Image component for automatic optimization (lazy loading, responsive sizes, WebP/AVIF format, blur placeholders).
issues.fix: When replacing placeholders with real images, use `import Image from 'next/image'` with appropriate width, height, and sizes props. Consider priority={true} for above-the-fold hero images.

issues.title: All product detail sections loaded eagerly
issues.description: ProductSections renders HeroSection, HighlightsSection, DescriptionSection, ScreenshotsSection, SpecsSection, SellerSection, ReviewsSection, and FaqSection all eagerly. Below-the-fold sections (ScreenshotsSection, ReviewsSection, FaqSection) could be lazy loaded to improve initial page load time.
issues.fix: Consider using React.lazy() + Suspense for below-the-fold sections, or use intersection observer-based loading. This becomes more important as product detail pages grow with real images and content.

issues.title: 18 Lucide icons imported statically in icon map
issues.description: The ICON_MAP imports 18 named Lucide icons statically. Lucide-react is tree-shakeable, so only these 18 are bundled, but they all load even when a product only uses 3-4 of them. This is a minor concern since each Lucide icon is small (~1KB).
issues.fix: Current approach is acceptable. The static map enables type-safe lookups and Lucide icons are lightweight. Dynamic imports would add complexity for minimal gain. No action needed unless the icon set grows significantly.

issues.title: Provider nesting depth is appropriate
issues.description: The Providers component nests 7 providers deep (NuqsAdapter > QueryProvider > MSWProvider > CartProvider > FlyToCartProvider > ErrorProvider). Each provider adds a React context layer. The current depth is reasonable and each provider serves a distinct purpose.
issues.fix: No action needed. The nesting is well-structured with CartProvider properly memoizing its context value.

issues.title: CartContext properly memoized with useCallback and useMemo
issues.description: CartProvider correctly uses useCallback for all dispatch wrappers (addItem, removeItem, updateQuantity, clearCart) and useMemo for the context value. This is good practice and prevents unnecessary re-renders of consumers that only use dispatch functions.
issues.fix: No action needed. This is well-implemented.

issues.title: Grid ordering properly memoized
issues.description: ProductGrid uses useMemo for buildGridOrder with correct dependencies [products, cols]. This prevents recalculation on unrelated re-renders.

issues.title: Dependencies are lean and well-chosen
issues.description: The store app has a focused dependency set with no obviously oversized or unused packages. @tanstack/react-table is listed but usage was not observed in current components -- it may be planned for future use.
issues.fix: Monitor @tanstack/react-table. If not used within the current milestone, consider removing it to reduce bundle size (~50KB). Otherwise, no action needed.
